diseases.name: Damping-off
diseases.causal_agent: Pythium spp., Phytophthora spp., Fusarium spp., Rhizoctonia spp., Sclerotium rolfsii
diseases.impact: Seedlings collapse suddenly at the base of the stem. Roots rot, leading to seedling death. Rhizoctonia spp. causes brown-black discoloration at the base.
diseases.solution: Prepare nursery beds in uncultivated, well-drained land. Sterilize nursery beds (burning, solarization, or chemicals). Remove infected plants and surrounding soil. Seed treatment (before sowing): Thiram 80% WP – 5g/kg, Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP – 4g/kg, Captan 50% WP – 6g/kg. Soil treatment (spot application around plant base): Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP – 50g/50l/10m2, Thiram 80% WP – 70g/50l/10m2, Captan 50% WP – 60g/50l/10m2.

diseases.name: Collar/Foot Rot
diseases.impact: Leaves turn yellow. Decay occurs in the collar region. Fungal mycelial growth or brown mycelial growth is present in the collar region.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants and surrounding soil. Improve drainage. Deep plowing and exposing infected soil to strong sunlight. Prevent root damage when transplanting seedlings. Soil treatment (spot application around plant base): Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP – 50g/50l/10m2, Thiram 80% WP – 70g/50l/10m2, Captan 50% WP – 60g/50l/10m2.

diseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
diseases.impact: Leaves turn yellow and wilt. Vascular tissue in the stem discolors. Roots rot, leading to plant death.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants and surrounding soil. Improve drainage. Deep plowing and exposing infected soil to strong sunlight. Prevent root damage when transplanting seedlings. Soil treatment (spot application around plant base): Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP – 50g/50l/10m2, Thiram 80% WP – 70g/50l/10m2, Captan 50% WP – 60g/50l/10m2.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Circular yellow patches or spots appear on the lower epidermis of leaves. White powdery residue is seen on the upper epidermis of leaves.
diseases.solution: Apply recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development. Sulfur 80% WG 50g/10l water, Chlorothalonil 500g/l SC - 30ml/10l water, Carbendazim 50% WP - 7g/10l water.

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.impact: Sunken circular spots with copper to orange-colored black concentric rings in the center. Pink conidia (spores) ooze from the lesions.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds. Avoid excessive irrigation. Remove and destroy infected fruits. Seed treatment (before sowing): Thiram 80% WP – 5g/kg, Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP – 4g/kg, Captan 50% WP – 6g/kg. Apply fungicides during flowering in rainy season: Flusilazole 500g/l SC – 10ml/10l water, Metiram 55% + Pyraclostrobin 5% WP – 20g/10l water, Carbendazim 50% WG – 7g/10l water, Thiophanate-methyl 70% WP – 6g/10l water, Chlorothalonil 500 SC – 30ml/10l water.

diseases.name: Phomopsis Blight
diseases.impact: Small gray canker spots with black margins appear on stems and veins. Centers of spots turn brown to black due to conidia development. Soft and watery fruit spots turn black and become mummified.
diseases.solution: Remove and destroy infected fruits. Apply recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development: Chlorothalonil 500g/l SC - 30ml/10l water, Carbendazim 50% WP - 7g/10l water, Thiophanate-methyl 70% WP 6g/10l water.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Single branches/stems show slight wilting symptoms. Rapid and permanent wilting of the entire plant. Vascular tissues discolor. When infected parts are cut and immersed in clear water, a thin viscous bacterial ooze emerges from the cut end.
diseases.solution: Remove and destroy infected plants along with soil. Destroy crop residues after harvest. Crop rotation with non-susceptible crops (cruciferous vegetables and okra) helps reduce disease. Use resistant varieties. Clean and disinfect all farm equipment/tools. No chemical control method.

diseases.name: Little Leaf Disease (witches’ broom)
diseases.impact: Leaves are narrow, soft, smooth, and yellow. Internodes of the stem shorten. Auxiliary buds enlarge but their petioles and leaves remain shortened, giving the plant a bushy appearance. Flowers remain green/no flowering. Fruiting is rare.
diseases.solution: Remove and burn infected plants. Control leafhopper (Hishimonus phycitiy) with recommended insecticides to minimize disease spread.
